titulo: 50 Reichspfennig — Woman Planting Oak Sapling (Weimar 1924)
Allegorie der Jus Civile (Christian Wenzinger)
descricao: Allegory of Civil Law (Jus Civile) by Christian Wenzinger, 1752, at a former Benedictine abbey. Distinction between civil and canonical law personified — Justice in her specifically secular legal form within an ecclesiastical setting.
citacao: WENZINGER, Christian. Allegorie der Jus Civile. 1752. 1 escultura. Sankt Peter, Benedictine Abbey. Disponível em: https://www.bildindex.de/document/obj20677105.

titulo: Barocke Holzdecke: Personifikation der Gerechtigkeit
descricao: Baroque wooden ceiling in a Lüneburg residential building with allegorical medallions: Personification of Justice. The female justice figure domesticated — appearing not only in courts and public buildings but in private domestic spaces.
citacao: PERSONIFIKATION der Gerechtigkeit (Barocke Holzdecke). 1660–1780. 1 medalhão em teto de madeira. Lüneburg, Große Bäckerstraße 12. Disponível em: https://www.bildindex.de/document/obj20672587.

titulo: Die Göttin der Gerechtigkeit nach dem heutigen Geschmacke
descricao: 'The Goddess of Justice according to contemporary taste' — an Enlightenment-era satirical engraving critiquing contemporary justice through its female allegorical form. Shows how the figure of Justice was deployed as a vehicle for political commentary.
citacao: RODE, Bernhard. Die Göttin der Gerechtigkeit nach dem heutigen Geschmacke. Século XVIII. 1 gravura. Kupferstich-Kabinett Dresden. Disponível em: https://www.bildindex.de/document/obj30110594.

titulo: Gerechtigkeit (Allegory of Justice) by Lucas Cranach the Elder
descricao: Painting showing the personification of Justice as a half-length nude female figure against dark background, holding sword and scales. Inscribed 'GERECHTIGKAEIT'. One of the most influential Renaissance depictions — Cranach's eroticized Justice figure set a visual template that persisted for centuries. Northern Renaissance.
citacao: CRANACH, Lucas, o Velho. Gerechtigkeit. 1537. 1 pintura, óleo sobre painel, 72 × 49,6 cm. Coleção particular. Disponível em: https://lucascranach.org/en/PRIVATE_NONE-P457/.

titulo: Germania
descricao: Personificação da Alemanha como mulher guerreira (Germania), pintada por Friedrich August von Kaulbach em agosto de 1914, no início da Primeira Guerra Mundial. A figura feminina erguida aparece com coroa imperial de estilo otoniano, espada desembainhada, escudo heráldico e couraça feminina, em fundo noturno com horizonte em chamas. Kaulbach representa a Alemanha em postura defensiva e bélica, simbolizando a nação armada e pronta para o combate. A obra foi encomendada como resposta visual ao clima de mobilização nacional alemã de agosto de 1914, funcionando como propaganda patriótica de alto re
direitos: Domínio público (Friedrich August von Kaulbach, 1850–1920; obra de 1914)
citacao: KAULBACH, Friedrich August von. Germania. 1914. 1 pintura (óleo sobre tela). Berlim: Deutsches Historisches Museum. Disponível em: https://www.dhm.de/lemo/bestand/objekt/gemaelde-friedrich-august-von-kaulbach-germania-1914. Acesso em: 28 mar. 2026.

titulo: Justitia (Hameln, Stiftsherrenhaus)
descricao: Justitia figure on the Stiftsherrenhaus (Canon's House) in Hamelin, c. 1558. A Weser Renaissance example of Justice as architectural ornament on a residential/ecclesiastical building.
citacao: JUSTITIA (Stiftsherrenhaus, Hameln). c. 1558. 1 escultura arquitetônica. Hameln, Osterstraße 8. Disponível em: https://www.bildindex.de/document/obj20608421.

titulo: Justitia (Jacob Matham)
descricao: Print depicting Justitia — the female allegorical figure of Justice with her traditional attributes (scales, sword). By Jacob Matham, a major Dutch engraver, student of Hendrick Goltzius.
citacao: MATHAM, Jacob. Justitia. c. 1600. 1 gravura. Herzog Anton Ulrich Museum. Disponível em: https://www.europeana.eu/en/item/89/item_ZCR4KC47OMJN7DRRDFTIWRQS33M7PBN2.

titulo: Justitia an der Rathausuhr (Esslingen)
descricao: Justitia figure on the town hall clock of Esslingen am Neckar (1589). Justice as a permanent civic presence on a public timepiece — marking the hours under the gaze of the female allegory of law.
citacao: JUSTITIA an der Rathausuhr, Esslingen am Neckar. 1589. 1 escultura. Altes Rathaus, Esslingen. Disponível em: https://www.bildindex.de/document/obj20553978.

titulo: Justitia, Allegory of Justice (Johann Oswald Harms)
descricao: Drawing depicting Justitia — allegorical female figure of justice with scales and sword. German Baroque representation of the legal feminine allegory.
citacao: HARMS, Johann Oswald. Justitia, allegory of justice. [séc. XVII]. 1 desenho. Herzog Anton Ulrich Museum. Disponível em: https://www.europeana.eu/en/item/89/item_MH6IXNHWTECBQCNPU7IIMCFP2BDFAV76.

titulo: Kamin mit Justitia (Leipzig, Altes Rathaus)
descricao: Fireplace with Justitia in the Leipzig Old Town Hall (c. 1610). Justice as a decorative element on a fireplace in the main council chamber — the warming presence of legal authority in the deliberative space.
citacao: KAMIN mit Justitia (Leipzig, Altes Rathaus). c. 1610. 1 decoração de lareira. Altes Rathaus, Leipzig. Disponível em: https://www.bildindex.de/document/obj30143134.

titulo: Relief: Gerechtigkeit und Eintracht (Bremen Rathaus)
descricao: Relief of Justice (Gerechtigkeit) and Concord (Eintracht) on the Bremen Old Town Hall (1596–1605). Female Justice paired with female Concord on a civic building — the visual argument that justice produces social harmony.
citacao: RELIEF: Gerechtigkeit und Eintracht. 1596–1605. 1 relevo. Altes Rathaus, Bremen. Disponível em: https://www.bildindex.de/document/obj20459153.

titulo: Thronende Justitia / Allegorie der strafenden Gerechtigkeit
descricao: Photograph of a ceiling fresco in Koblenz showing Justitia enthroned — the allegorical female figure of criminal justice. A German example of Justice as a seated female sovereign figure, wielding punitive authority from a throne-like position.
citacao: ZICK, Januarius. Thronende Justitia / Allegorie der strafenden Gerechtigkeit. [séc. XVIII]. Fotografia de Schulze-Marburg, Rudolf, 1943-1944. Zentralinstitut für Kunstgeschichte. Disponível em: https://www.europeana.eu/en/item/201/item_IBA5QWMUFFBZOLE2SPIJO5QD2SPIZS6S.

titulo: A Justiça (Alfredo Ceschiatti, STF Brasília)
instituicao: Supremo Tribunal Federal, Praça dos Três Poderes, Brasília
descricao: Monumental granite sculpture of Justice (Themis) seated before the Supreme Federal Tribunal in Brasília. Eyes closed, holding a sword with both hands. Designed to complement Oscar Niemeyer's modernist architecture. The Brazilian Justiça — a seated, blindfolded, sword-bearing woman as the face of the highest court. Attacked on January 8, 2023, making it a central object in the dissertation's analysis of iconoclasm and legal literacy.
citacao: CESCHIATTI, Alfredo. A Justiça. 1961. 1 escultura em granito. Supremo Tribunal Federal, Praça dos Três Poderes, Brasília. Disponível em: https://acervos.ims.com.br/index.php/Detail/objects/18758.

titulo: Alegoria da República (Décio Villares)
descricao: Allegorical painting of the Brazilian Republic by Décio Villares, the positivist artist who designed the blue disc on Brazil's national flag. The female figure wears a Phrygian cap inscribed with 'Ordem e Progresso' — directly linking republican feminine allegory to positivist ideology. Key work for understanding how European allegorical forms were transplanted and adapted in Brazilian republican iconography.
citacao: VILLARES, Décio. Alegoria da República. c. 1900. 1 pintura, óleo sobre tela, 97 x 77 cm. Museu Histórico Nacional. Disponível em: https://archive.nyu.edu/handle/2451/61396.

titulo: Detalhe do monumento à República, Praça da República, Belém
descricao: Close-up detail of female allegorical figure holding a flag, standing on a lion. Part of the Monument to the Republic in Praça da República, Belém. The female figure personifies the Brazilian Republic with classical republican iconography — flag, lion (strength), classical garb.
citacao: MONUMENTO à República, Praça da República, Belém. 1940. 1 fotografia, p&b. In: Álbum de fotografias da visita de Getúlio Vargas a Belém. Museu da República/IBRAM. Disponível em: https://brasilianafotografica.bn.gov.br/brasiliana/handle/20.500.12156.1/13609.

titulo: Eliseu Visconti — Alegoria (Biblioteca Nacional, Rio de Janeiro)
instituicao: Fundação Biblioteca Nacional, Rio de Janeiro
descricao: Impressionist-style allegorical panel for the Presidential Room of the Biblioteca Nacional in Rio de Janeiro. Female allegorical figure in Visconti's distinctive impressionist idiom — the French-trained Brazilian artist adapting European allegorical vocabulary to national institutional decoration. A key example of transatlantic visual transfer.
citacao: VISCONTI, Eliseu. Alegoria (Biblioteca Nacional). 1910. 1 painel decorativo, óleo. Fundação Biblioteca Nacional, Rio de Janeiro. Disponível em: https://eliseuvisconti.com.br/obra/d702/.

titulo: Estátua feminina na abertura da amostra dos produtos do Rio Grande do Sul
descricao: Black and white photograph showing a female statue displayed at the National Exhibition of 1908. Female allegorical figure representing progress and abundance at the Rio Grande do Sul exhibition pavilion.
citacao: BOSCAGLI, José. [Estátua feminina na abertura da amostra dos produtos do Rio Grande do Sul]. 1908. 1 fotografia, p&b, 16,5 x 23 cm. Museu Histórico Nacional. Disponível em: https://brasilianafotografica.bn.gov.br/brasiliana/handle/20.500.12156.1/11778.

titulo: Jardim do Campo da Aclamação (Campo de Santana): estátuas do Outono e da Primavera
descricao: Sepia photograph showing female allegorical statues representing the seasons of Autumn and Spring in Campo de Santana (formerly Campo da Aclamação), Rio de Janeiro. Female figures as seasonal allegories in a republican public space — note the location at Praça da República.
citacao: SANTOS, Guilherme. Jardim do Campo da Aclamação, atual Campo de Santana; estátuas do Outono e da Primavera. c. 1925. 1 fotografia, gelatina/prata, sépia, 4,5 x 11 cm. Instituto Moreira Salles. Disponível em: https://brasilianafotografica.bn.gov.br/brasiliana/handle/20.500.12156.1/11529.

titulo: Monumento à República, Praça da República, Belém (vista geral)
descricao: Full view of Monument to the Republic with female allegorical figure atop a column in Praça da República, Belém. The towering female figure dominates the urban landscape, arms raised, embodying the Republic.
citacao: MONUMENTO à República, Praça da República, Belém. 1940. 1 fotografia, p&b. In: Álbum de fotografias da visita de Getúlio Vargas a Belém. Museu da República/IBRAM. Disponível em: https://brasilianafotografica.bn.gov.br/brasiliana/handle/20.500.12156.1/13611.

titulo: Justitia civilis (Maarten de Vos)
descricao: Allegorical depiction of civil justice (Justitia civilis) by Flemish master Maarten de Vos. Distinction between civil and criminal justice personified as separate female figures — important for understanding the multiplication of legal feminine allegories.
citacao: VOS, Maarten de. Justitia civilis. [séc. XVI]. Royal Institute for Cultural Heritage. Disponível em: https://www.europeana.eu/en/item/2048001/AP_10133590.

titulo: Palais de Justice de Bruxelles (Joseph Poelaert)
descricao: The Brussels Palace of Justice — one of the largest courthouses ever built and a monument to 19th-century legal grandeur. The 'pompous staircase with sculptures attired in Roman robes' and allegorical female figures throughout. Poelaert's design made the Belgian state visible through classical feminine allegory. Central to the dissertation's Belgian case study and Huygebaert's colonial paternalism analysis.
citacao: POELAERT, Joseph. Palais de Justice de Bruxelles. 1866–1883. Complexo arquitetônico. Bruxelles. Disponível em: https://be-monumen.be/patrimoine-belge/poelaert-joseph/.

titulo: Time and Justice Reveal the Truth
descricao: Allegorical depiction showing Time and Justice revealing Truth. Belgian example of the Justice-Truth triad where Justice is personified as a woman who, together with Time, unveils Truth — a visual argument for the transparency of law.
citacao: WALKER, Johan. Time and justice reveal the truth. [s.d.]. Royal Institute for Cultural Heritage. Disponível em: https://www.europeana.eu/en/item/2048001/AP_10342206.

titulo: America pois'd in the balance of justice
descricao: Revolutionary-era print showing two despondent women (possibly Britannia and America) in a rugged landscape, with ships and a burning city. The balance of justice as a geopolitical metaphor during the American Revolution.
citacao: AMERICA pois'd in the balance of justice. 1776. 1 gravura. Library of Congress, Washington, D.C. Disponível em: https://www.loc.gov/item/2004673327/.

titulo: Behold! a fabric now to freedom rear'd (Constitutional allegory)
descricao: Allegorical scene celebrating the US Constitution: Concord standing, Clio writing, Cupid holding the 'Constitution' before a pillar labeled 'Sacred to Liberty Justice and Peace' with thirteen columns. Female allegories deployed to sanctify the constitutional founding.
citacao: TRENCHARD, James. Behold! a fabric now to freedom rear'd. 1788. 1 gravura. Library of Congress, Washington, D.C. Disponível em: https://www.loc.gov/item/2004676797/.

titulo: Columbia calls—Enlist now for U.S. Army
descricao: WWI recruitment poster (1917) depicting Columbia standing on the globe atop North America, holding the American flag and a sword, calling men to enlist. Designed by Frances Adams Halsted (who also wrote the accompanying poem) and painted by Vincente Aderente. After the U.S. entered WWI on April 6, 1917, Halsted donated the design to the War Department; the New York Times planned 500,000 copies. A canonical example of Columbia as female national allegory in wartime American visual culture.
citacao: HALSTED, Frances Adams (des.); ADERENTE, Vincente (paint.). Columbia calls—Enlist now for U.S. Army. 1917. 1 litografia colorida (cartaz). Library of Congress, Washington, D.C. Disponível em: https://www.loc.gov/item/95506508/.

titulo: Columbia presenting sword to Admiral Dewey
descricao: Columbia, the female personification of America, presenting a sword and crowning Admiral Dewey after the Spanish-American War. The martial dimension of the national female allegory — Columbia as warrior-queen bestowing military honors.
citacao: COLUMBIA presenting sword to Admiral Dewey. 1899. 1 cromolitogravura. Library of Congress, Washington, D.C. Disponível em: https://www.loc.gov/item/2018697020/.

titulo: Die Wage der Macht / La balance de la puissance
descricao: Justice holding scales on which Britannia outweighs a Spaniard, Frenchman, America (as Native), and Dutchman. Justice as a geopolitical arbiter — weighing national power through the female allegorical figure. Bilingual (German/French) print.
citacao: WILL, Johann Martin. Die Wage der Macht / La balance de la puissance. 1780. 1 gravura. Library of Congress, Washington, D.C. Disponível em: https://www.loc.gov/item/2004673370/.

titulo: Foire France-Américaine: Justice, Liberty, Demeter, Athena
descricao: WWI-era Franco-American poster showing four allegorical figures: Justice, Liberty (with American flag), Demeter, and Athena above Saint-Sulpice. The transatlantic circulation of female legal allegories during wartime.
citacao: ROMBERG, Maurice. Foire France-Américaine: Justice, Liberty, Demeter, Athena. 1917. 1 litogravura colorida. Library of Congress, Washington, D.C. Disponível em: https://www.loc.gov/item/99613542/.

titulo: Hedwig Reicher as Columbia in suffrage pageant
descricao: German actress Hedwig Reicher in costume as 'Columbia' at a suffrage pageant where Columbia summoned Justice, Charity, Liberty, Peace, and Hope to revive the crusade of women. A feminist reappropriation of national female allegory — Columbia mobilized for women's suffrage.
citacao: HEDWIG Reicher as Columbia in suffrage pageant. 1913. 1 fotografia. Library of Congress, Washington, D.C. Disponível em: https://www.loc.gov/item/97510759/.

titulo: Justice (blindfolded woman with sword and scales)
descricao: Official US government steel engraving of blindfolded Justice holding sword and scales. An institutional production of the legal allegory — the state manufacturing its own symbolic self-representation.
citacao: JUSTICE (mulher vendada com espada e balança). [s.d.]. 1 gravura em aço. US Bureau of Printing & Engraving. Library of Congress, Washington, D.C. Disponível em: https://www.loc.gov/item/2007675578/.

titulo: Justice and History (plaster model for US Capitol)
suporte: Salted paper print of sculpture maquette
descricao: Photograph of plaster model for 'Justice and History' by Thomas Crawford, designed for the Senate bronze doors at the East Portico of the US Capitol. Justice and History as paired female figures guarding the entrance to the legislative chamber.
citacao: CRAWFORD, Thomas. Justice and History (modelo para o Capitólio dos EUA). c. 1860. 1 fotografia (salt print). Library of Congress, Washington, D.C. Disponível em: https://www.loc.gov/item/2009631377/.

titulo: Temple of Liberty
descricao: Woodcut broadside by Jared W. Bell (1834) depicting the Temple of Liberty as an allegorical female figure — the Goddess of Liberty — enthroned or standing within a classical temple. A key example of antebellum American female allegorical imagery deploying classical architecture and female personification to represent republican values and national identity.
citacao: BELL, Jared W. Temple of liberty. 1834. 1 xilogravura. Library of Congress, Washington, D.C. Disponível em: https://www.loc.gov/item/2008661765/.

titulo: The Goddess of Liberty (woman in star-spangled cape)
descricao: Photograph of a woman model in Liberty costume with stars, representing Liberty/Columbia during the Civil War era. The living body performing the allegory — a real woman embodying the national feminine symbol.
citacao: THE GODDESS of Liberty (mulher com capa estrelada). 1861. 1 fotografia (carte de visite). Library of Congress, Washington, D.C. Disponível em: https://www.loc.gov/item/2018664118/.

titulo: Wake up America! Civilization calls every man, woman and child!
descricao: WWI recruitment poster by James Montgomery Flagg (1917) depicting Columbia — the female personification of America — sleeping, dressed in patriotic stars and stripes and wearing a Phrygian cap. The figure of Columbia, modeled on actress Mary Arthur, embodies the nation's allegorical identity. One of the most iconic American female allegorical images of the First World War, later reproduced as one of the most widely distributed WWI posters. Published for 'Wake Up, America' Day, April 19, 1917.
citacao: FLAGG, James Montgomery. Wake up America! Civilization calls every man, woman and child! 1917. 1 litografia colorida (cartaz). Library of Congress, Washington, D.C. Disponível em: https://www.loc.gov/item/91726511/.

titulo: Alegoría de la Primera República española (La Flaca)
descricao: Ilustração satírica publicada na revista republicana La Flaca (Barcelona, 1869–1873), em período correspondente à Primeira República espanhola (fev. 1873 – dez. 1874). Tomás Padró i Pedret, ilustrador principal da publicação, representou a República española como figura feminina, frequentemente com barrete frígio, acompanhada de líderes políticos como Castelar, Pi y Margall, Figueras e Salmerón. La Flaca foi o principal veículo de iconografia republicana federal durante o Sexênio Democrático, e suas alegorias femininas da Espanha constituem corpus fundamental para a história da iconografia pol
direitos: Domínio público (Tomás Padró i Pedret, 1840–1877; publicação de 1873)
citacao: PADRÓ I PEDRET, Tomás. Alegoría de la Primera República española. La Flaca, Barcelona, 1873. 1 litografia. Madri: Biblioteca Nacional de España, Hemeroteca Digital. Disponível em: https://hemerotecadigital.bne.es/hd/es/card?sid=3971781. Acesso em: 28 mar. 2026.

titulo: Science Presenting Steam and Electricity to Commerce and Manufacture ($2 Silver Certificate)
autoria: Edwin Howland Blashfield (artist), George F.C. Smillie (engraver)

titulo: Allégorie pour le frontispice du compte rendu au Roi par M. Necker (variante)
descricao: Second variant of the allegorical engraving designed as frontispiece for Necker's 'Compte rendu au Roi' (1781). Female allegorical figures in a variant compositional arrangement. Documents the multiple print versions in circulation around this seminal text on royal finances and public accountability.
citacao: ALLÉGORIE pour le frontispice du compte rendu au Roi par M. Necker (variante). 1781. 1 estampe. Bibliothèque nationale de France. Disponível em: https://gallica.bnf.fr/ark:/12148/btv1b6942595s.

titulo: Allégorie pour le frontispice du compte-rendu au Roi par M. Necker
descricao: Engraved allegorical frontispiece designed for Necker's 'Compte rendu au Roi' (1781), the public financial report that became a landmark in Ancien Régime transparency. Female allegorical figures — variously interpreted as Justice, Abundance, and Truth — frame the royal accounting. An early instance of female allegory deployed in the visual rhetoric of fiscal governance.
citacao: ALLÉGORIE pour le frontispice du compte-rendu au Roi par M. Necker. 1781. 1 estampe. Bibliothèque nationale de France. Disponível em: https://gallica.bnf.fr/ark:/12148/btv1b8410270p.

titulo: Allégorie pour servir de frontispice au compte-rendu au Roi par M. Necker
descricao: Third allegorical engraving serving as frontispiece for Necker's 'Compte rendu au Roi' (1781). This variant reinforces the corpus of female allegorical imagery mobilized around the question of royal fiscal accountability in the late Ancien Régime. The three Necker frontispieces (FR-015, FR-016, FR-017) together document the visual politics of the transparency debate.
citacao: ALLÉGORIE pour servir de frontispice au compte-rendu au Roi par M. Necker. 1781. 1 estampe. Bibliothèque nationale de France. Disponível em: https://gallica.bnf.fr/ark:/12148/btv1b8410268m.

titulo: Buste de la République (Agence Rol)
descricao: Press photograph of a bust sculpture representing the French Republic. Busts of Marianne became ubiquitous in French town halls (mairies) under the Third Republic — the multiplication of the female allegorical form across thousands of civic spaces.
citacao: BUSTE de la République. [s.d.]. 1 fotografia de imprensa. Agence Rol. Bibliothèque nationale de France. Disponível em: https://www.europeana.eu/en/item/9200518/ark__12148_btv1b6952880n.

titulo: Decretum Gratiani (manuscrito iluminado, BnF)
autoria: Graciano (Gratianus) [autor do texto]; iluminador bolonhês anônimo
descricao: Manuscrito iluminado do Decretum Gratiani (Concordia discordantium canonum, c. 1140), coletânea de direito canônico compilada pelo monge Graciano em Bolonha, conservado na Bibliothèque nationale de France. Os manuscritos do Decretum são célebres por suas miniaturas iluminadas — especialmente capitais historiadas e frontispícios — que representam a Justitia como figura feminina com espada e balança, cenas de julgamento e disputas jurídicas. Produzido nos scriptoria bolonheses no século XIII, o exemplar da BnF integra a tradição dos manuscritos jurídicos iluminados que constituem o principal cor
direitos: Domínio público (obra medieval); digitalização BnF — Gallica (acesso aberto)
citacao: GRACIANO. Decretum Gratiani (Concordia discordantium canonum). [Bolonha], séc. XIII. 1 manuscrito iluminado, pergaminho. Paris: Bibliothèque nationale de France. Disponível em: https://gallica.bnf.fr/ark:/12148/btv1b10544534k.item. Acesso em: 28 mar. 2026.

titulo: Déclaration des droits de l'homme et du citoyen
autoria: Le Barbier l'aîné (des.); L. Laurent (grav.); Dien (grav.)
descricao: Engraved broadside of the Declaration of the Rights of Man and Citizen (1789), designed by Le Barbier l'aîné and engraved by Laurent and Dien. The composition frames the text of the Declaration with two female allegorical figures — Liberty with Phrygian cap and Justice with scales — flanking a radiating triangle (Eye of Providence). Canonical image of Revolutionary visual rhetoric fusing law, allegory, and print.
citacao: LE BARBIER L'AÎNÉ (des.); LAURENT, L. (grav.); DIEN (grav.). Déclaration des droits de l'homme et du citoyen. 1789. 1 estampe. Bibliothèque nationale de France. Disponível em: https://gallica.bnf.fr/ark:/12148/btv1b69480451.

titulo: Déclaration des droits de l'homme et du citoyen (variante)
descricao: Anonymous engraved broadside variant of the Declaration of the Rights of Man and Citizen (1789). Presents the text of the Declaration within an allegorical decorative framework featuring female figures. Variant edition circulating alongside the canonical Le Barbier design, reflecting the proliferation of allegorical print culture during the Revolution.
citacao: DÉCLARATION des droits de l'homme et du citoyen (variante). 1789. 1 estampe. Bibliothèque nationale de France. Disponível em: https://gallica.bnf.fr/ark:/12148/btv1b8410817c.

titulo: Female Allegory (Pierre Puvis de Chavannes)
descricao: Female allegorical figure by Pierre Puvis de Chavannes, the great French muralist whose public commissions (Sorbonne, Panthéon, Hôtel de Ville) defined the visual language of Third Republic civic spaces. This drawing connects to the larger programme of republican allegorical decoration.
citacao: PUVIS DE CHAVANNES, Pierre. Female allegory. [séc. XIX]. 1 desenho. Albertina Museum. Disponível em: https://www.europeana.eu/en/item/15508/DG1918_7.

titulo: Journée du Poilu, 25 et 26 décembre 1915, organisée par le Parlement
descricao: WWI charity poster by Steinlen (1915) for the 'Journée du Poilu' (Soldier's Day, December 25–26), organized by the French Parliament to raise funds for soldiers at the front. The composition depicts a French soldier in the foreground, with a female figure — embodying the Republic/Marianne — in an embrace or gesture of support behind him. One of Steinlen's key contributions to French WWI visual culture, combining social realism with Republican allegorical tradition.
citacao: STEINLEN, Théophile Alexandre. Journée du Poilu, 25 et 26 décembre 1915, organisée par le Parlement. 1915. 1 affiche (litografia). Bibliothèque nationale de France. Disponível em: https://gallica.bnf.fr/ark:/12148/btv1b9012753r.

titulo: L'envoyé de la Justice
descricao: Engraving titled 'The Envoy of Justice' — depicting a figure sent by Justice (as a female allegorical power). Early example in the series of Justice representations by Chifflart.
citacao: CHIFFLART, François-Nicolas. L'envoyé de la Justice. 1859. 1 gravura. Bibliothèque nationale de France. Disponível em: https://gallica.bnf.fr/ark:/12148/btv1b53173732g.

titulo: La Justice, la Vengeance et la Vérité
descricao: Engraving depicting three female allegorical figures: Justice, Vengeance, and Truth. A powerful composition showing Justice as a dominant female presence, flanked by Truth and divine Vengeance — key triad in French republican allegorical vocabulary.
citacao: CHIFFLART, François-Nicolas. La Justice, la Vengeance et la Vérité. 1865. 1 gravura. Bibliothèque nationale de France. Disponível em: https://gallica.bnf.fr/ark:/12148/btv1b531737635.

titulo: La Liberté guidant le peuple (Eugène Delacroix)
descricao: 'Liberty Leading the People' — the iconic image of the July Revolution of 1830. A bare-breasted female figure (Liberty/Marianne) leads armed citizens over barricades, holding the tricolour flag and a musket. The most powerful visual crystallization of the republic-as-woman — Warner's central case study, Agulhon's key image. Foundational for the entire Iconocracy project.
citacao: DELACROIX, Eugène. La Liberté guidant le peuple. 1830. 1 pintura, óleo sobre tela, 260 × 325 cm. Musée du Louvre, Paris. Disponível em: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Eug%C3%A8ne_Delacroix_-_Le_28_Juillet._La_Libert%C3%A9_guidant_le_peuple.jpg.

titulo: La République aimable (Félicien Rops)
descricao: Satirical engraving by Belgian-French artist Félicien Rops depicting 'The Amiable Republic' — a subversive or eroticized representation of the Republic as a woman. Critical for understanding the contested gendered nature of republican allegory and its use in satirical press.
citacao: ROPS, Félicien. La République aimable. 1871. 1 gravura. Bibliothèque nationale de France. Disponível em: https://gallica.bnf.fr/ark:/12148/btv1b531842166.

titulo: La République nous appelle... (Steinlen)
descricao: WWI recruitment poster: 'The Republic calls us...' — Marianne as a summoning female figure, calling citizens to arms. Steinlen's powerful composition shows the Republic-as-woman as a quasi-maternal/military figure demanding sacrifice.
citacao: STEINLEN, Théophile Alexandre. La République nous appelle. 1915. 1 litografia. Bibliothèque nationale de France. Disponível em: https://www.europeana.eu/en/item/9200518/ark__12148_btv1b10510623s.

titulo: Le hochet de la République (Jean Veber)
descricao: Satirical print 'The Rattle of the Republic' by Jean Veber. The Republic as a woman reduced to a toy or rattle — a critical/ironic commentary on the infantilization or trivialization of republican symbolism through its female form.
citacao: VEBER, Jean. Le hochet de la République. [s.d.]. 1 gravura. Bibliothèque nationale de France. Disponível em: https://www.europeana.eu/en/item/9200518/ark__12148_btv1b8577646g.

titulo: Le triomphe de la Justice et de la Vérité
descricao: Engraving showing the triumph of Justice and Truth as female allegorical figures. Complete state with full lettering. Part of Chifflart's series on juridical allegory.
citacao: CHIFFLART, François-Nicolas. Le triomphe de la Justice et de la Vérité. 1865. 1 gravura. Bibliothèque nationale de France. Disponível em: https://gallica.bnf.fr/ark:/12148/btv1b531737529.

titulo: Marianne enseignant l'alphabet à des enfants (naïades et Marianne)
descricao: Engravings depicting naiads and Marianne teaching the alphabet to children. Powerful image of the Republic-as-woman as educator — embodying the republican ideal of laïcité and public instruction through a female allegorical figure.
citacao: ESTAMPES représentant des naïades et Marianne enseignant l'alphabet à des enfants. [1859-1910]. Bibliothèque nationale de France. Disponível em: https://gallica.bnf.fr/ark:/12148/btv1b10574064v.

titulo: République Française: 3e Emprunt de la Défense nationale
descricao: WWI propaganda poster for the 3rd National Defense loan. Marianne stands triumphantly above soldiers, embodying the Republic rallying her citizens to war. The female allegory mobilized for military propaganda — a critical junction of gender, state power, and visual persuasion.
direitos: No Copyright – Other Known Legal Restrictions
citacao: LELONG, René. République Française: 3e Emprunt de la Défense nationale. 1917. 1 cartaz. Bibliothèque nationale de France. Disponível em: https://www.europeana.eu/en/item/9200518/ark__12148_btv1b10051217v.

titulo: Semeuse 1 Franc silver coin (Oscar Roty)
The French Crisis: Madame La République
descricao: Political cartoon from 1887 showing 'Madame La République' during the French political crisis. The Republic personified as a woman under stress — a key document of how female allegory was deployed during political instability.
citacao: THE FRENCH crisis: Madame La République. 1887. 1 gravura. Bibliothèque nationale de France. Disponível em: https://gallica.bnf.fr/ark:/12148/btv1b8438243t.

titulo: [Liberté, Égalité, Fraternité — portrait allégorique d'Ernouf]
descricao: Revolutionary allegorical print combining the motto Liberté, Égalité, Fraternité with a portrait of Ernouf. Designed by Garneray and engraved by Quéverdo, this composite image exemplifies the visual culture of the Directory period, deploying female allegorical figures to embody Republican values alongside the honored portrait. Documents the consolidation of Marianne iconography in post-Terror Republican print culture.
citacao: GARNERAY (des.); QUÉVERDO (grav.). [Liberté, Égalité, Fraternité — portrait allégorique d'Ernouf]. c. 1793–1800. 1 estampe. Bibliothèque nationale de France. Disponível em: https://gallica.bnf.fr/ark:/12148/btv1b8412560z.

titulo: Allegorie der Justitia und eine weitere weibliche Allegorie
descricao: Italian drawing or print showing Justitia alongside another female allegorical figure. Early 16th-century example of the pairing of Justice with other virtues (Prudence, Temperance, Fortitude) — the four cardinal virtues personified as women.
citacao: ALLEGORIE der Justitia und eine weitere weibliche Allegorie. 1501–1550. 1 desenho/gravura. Museo Civico, Bassano del Grappa. Disponível em: https://www.bildindex.de/document/obj08108743.

titulo: Allegorie: Justitia (Marcantonio Raimondi)
descricao: Engraving of Justitia by Marcantonio Raimondi, the most important Italian engraver of the High Renaissance (pupil of Raphael's circle). This print disseminated the Italian Renaissance model of female Justice across Europe.
citacao: RAIMONDI, Marcantonio. Allegorie: Justitia. Início do século XVI. 1 gravura. Bibliotheca Hertziana, Roma. Disponível em: https://www.bildindex.de/document/obj08158050.

titulo: Allegory of Justice (Charles Audran after B.P. Cortona)
descricao: Allegorical representation of Justice, engraved by Charles Audran after an invention by Pietro da Cortona (Berrettini). Italian Baroque model of Justice as a female figure — one of the visual templates that circulated across Europe and eventually reached the Americas.
citacao: AUDRAN, Charles. Allegory of Justice. [séc. XVII]. 1 gravura. Casanatense Library. Disponível em: https://www.europeana.eu/en/item/446/RML0348941.

titulo: Justitia (marble head from Porta di Capua)
descricao: Marble head of Justitia from the Porta di Capua, built under Emperor Frederick II. One of the earliest surviving medieval personifications of Justice as a woman — predating the Renaissance tradition. Connected to the Hohenstaufen revival of Roman legal imagery.
citacao: JUSTITIA (cabeça em mármore da Porta di Capua). 1239–1240. 1 escultura em mármore. Museo Provinciale Campano, Capua. Disponível em: https://www.bildindex.de/document/obj08148970.

titulo: [Virtudes cardinales: Justica] (La Patria Ilustrada)
descricao: Litografia de José Guadalupe Posada publicada na revista mexicana La Patria Ilustrada (Cidade do México, 18 fev. 1889), integrante da série 'Virtudes cardinales' (Virtudes cardinais). A imagem representa a Justiça (Justica/Justitia) como figura feminina alegórica, retomando a iconografia greco-romana da Iustitia em contexto da imprensa ilustrada porfirista. Posada, o mais importante gravador popular mexicano, trabalhou para diversas publicações periódicas e produziu um vocabulário iconográfico de grande alcance para o imaginário político e moral mexicano. O exemplar é conservado na Yale Univer
direitos: Domínio público (José Guadalupe Posada, 1852–1913; publicação de 1889)
citacao: POSADA, José Guadalupe. [Virtudes cardinales: Justica]. La Patria Ilustrada, Cidade do México, 18 fev. 1889. 1 litografia, 23,5 × 16 cm. Washington: Library of Congress. Disponível em: https://www.loc.gov/item/2003656173/. Acesso em: 28 mar. 2026.

titulo: Allegorie op de rechtvaardigheid (Allegory of Justice, enthroned)
descricao: Title page with enthroned Justice (Rechtvaardigheid) on a pedestal. At her feet, attributes symbolizing worldly power. The throne-composition as a visual argument for the sovereignty of law over political authority.
citacao: ALLEGORIE op de rechtvaardigheid. Século XVII. 1 gravura (página de título). Rijksmuseum, Amsterdam. Disponível em: https://www.rijksmuseum.nl/en/collection/object/Allegorie-op-de-rechtvaardigheid--a26144337b14ab57eb5ca23025308032.

titulo: Justitia (bust with crown and eye pendant)
descricao: Bust of a woman with a crown and a necklace bearing an eye pendant, identified in inscription as Justitia. The all-seeing eye as a Justice attribute — surveillance and omniscience as female qualities of law.
citacao: JUSTITIA (busto com coroa e olho pendente). [s.d.]. 1 escultura (busto). Rijksmuseum, Amsterdam. Disponível em: https://www.rijksmuseum.nl/en/collection/object/Justitia--314f580f935ba978fb199ce7abeb0db1.

titulo: Rechtvaardigheid (Justitia)
descricao: Engraving depicting Justice (Justitia) as a female allegorical figure, attributed to Jacob Matham after a design by Hendrick Goltzius. Part of a series on the virtues. Justitia shown standing, holding a sword and scales, blindfolded.
citacao: MATHAM, Jacob (attr.). Rechtvaardigheid (Justitia). 1585–1589. 1 gravura. Rijksmuseum, Amsterdam. Disponível em: https://www.rijksmuseum.nl/en/collection/RP-P-OB-27.296.

titulo: Rechtvaardigheid (Justitia)
descricao: Engraving of Justice (Justitia) as a female allegorical figure, made by Jacob Matham after Hendrick Goltzius in 1597. Part of the series depicting the Four Cardinal Virtues. Justitia stands with sword and scales.
citacao: MATHAM, Jacob. Rechtvaardigheid (Justitia). 1597. 1 gravura. Rijksmuseum, Amsterdam. Disponível em: https://www.rijksmuseum.nl/en/collection/RP-P-OB-27.124.

titulo: Rechtvaardigheid (Justitia)
descricao: Engraving of Justice (Rechtvaardigheid / Justitia) by Jacob Matham, 1593. Female allegorical figure in classical dress, holding the sword and scales — canonical attributes of Justitia in Northern European legal iconography of the late 16th century.
citacao: MATHAM, Jacob. Rechtvaardigheid (Justitia). 1593. 1 gravura. Rijksmuseum, Amsterdam. Disponível em: https://www.rijksmuseum.nl/en/collection/RP-P-1887-A-12280.

titulo: Rechtvaardigheid (Justitia) / Iustitia
autoria: Anoniem, naar Hendrick Goltzius / Jacob Matham
descricao: Anonymous engraving of Justice (Iustitia) after the design tradition of Hendrick Goltzius and Jacob Matham. Female allegorical figure representing Justitia with the traditional attributes of sword and scales. Undated impression from the period 1585–1637.
citacao: ANONIEM. Rechtvaardigheid (Justitia) / Iustitia. 1585–1637. 1 gravura. Rijksmuseum, Amsterdam. Disponível em: https://www.rijksmuseum.nl/en/collection/RP-P-1882-A-6499.

titulo: Vrouwe Justitia (nude, with sword and scales)
descricao: 16th-century engraving of Vrouwe Justitia — nude, holding sword in right hand, scales at left, appearing to trample a child underfoot. A muscular, powerful rendering of female Justice — far from the idealized classical model.
citacao: VROUWE Justitia (nua, com espada e balança). 1500–1600. 1 gravura. Rijksmuseum, Amsterdam. Disponível em: https://www.rijksmuseum.nl/en/collection/object/Vrouwe-Justitia--13997b5b276f9bbbb36a847da4c3022a.

titulo: Vrouwe Justitia voor het oordeel van Zaleucus en het oordeel van Cambyses
descricao: Engraving by Willem Jacobsz Delff depicting Lady Justice (Vrouwe Justitia) presiding over the Judgment of Zaleucus and the Judgment of Cambyses — two classical exempla of justice. Female allegorical figure with sword and scales flanked by historical judicial scenes.
citacao: DELFF, Willem Jacobsz. Vrouwe Justitia voor het oordeel van Zaleucus en het oordeel van Cambyses. 1590–1638. 1 gravura. Rijksmuseum, Amsterdam. Disponível em: https://www.rijksmuseum.nl/en/collection/RP-P-1883-A-6992.

titulo: Alegoria ao Tempo (Pierre Antoine Quillard)
descricao: Allegorical engraving representing Time, companion piece to the Alegoria à Morte. Part of Quillard's allegorical series produced in Lisbon. Time as a personified figure — part of the broader iconographic programme where abstract concepts (Justice, Time, Truth, Death) take human form.
citacao: QUILLARD, Pierre Antoine. Alegoria ao Tempo. 1730. 1 gravura. Biblioteca Nacional de Portugal. Disponível em: https://bndigital.bnportugal.gov.pt/records/item/37507-alegoria-ao-tempo.

titulo: Alegoria à História (female allegorical engraving)
descricao: Historical allegorical engraving featuring female figures representing History. An early modern Portuguese example of the female allegorical tradition — History personified as a woman, part of the same visual vocabulary that produced Justice and Republic figures.
citacao: [ALEGORIA à História]. c. 1600. 1 gravura, água-forte, p&b. Biblioteca Nacional de Portugal. Disponível em: https://bndigital.bnportugal.gov.pt/records/item/39213-alegoria-a-historia.

titulo: Alegoria à Morte (Pierre Antoine Quillard)
descricao: Allegorical engraving representing Death, part of a series of allegorical works by the French-Portuguese artist Quillard produced in Lisbon. Demonstrates the French-Portuguese artistic exchange in allegorical visual culture during the Baroque period.
citacao: QUILLARD, Pierre Antoine. Alegoria à Morte. 1730. 1 gravura. Biblioteca Nacional de Portugal. Disponível em: https://bndigital.bnportugal.gov.pt/records/item/37491-alegoria-a-morte.

titulo: Justiça popular (UDP political poster)
descricao: Political poster from the Portuguese revolutionary period (post-Carnation Revolution, 1974): 'Popular Justice: the PIDE agent who murdered Dias Coelho will be judged at the people's cost!' Justice invoked in revolutionary political context — a moment when the concept of Justice was being radically renegotiated.
direitos: Copyrighted – accessible only in BNP internal network
citacao: UNIÃO DEMOCRÁTICA POPULAR. Justiça popular: o Pide que assassinou Dias Coelho vai ser julgado nas costas do povo! [1975?]. 1 cartaz. Biblioteca Nacional de Portugal. Disponível em: https://bndigital.bnportugal.gov.pt/records/item/17148-justica-popular.

titulo: Justitia and Prudentia (female personifications)
descricao: Justice and Prudence, female personifications, whole-length, in niches, with attributes (sword and tablets of the law; mirror and snake). The cardinal-virtue pairing of Justice and Prudence as women.
citacao: JUSTITIA and Prudentia (personificações femininas). [s.d.]. 1 gravura. British Museum, London. Disponível em: https://www.britishmuseum.org/collection/object/P_1862-0712-305.

titulo: Justitia enthroned, with sword and scales
descricao: Interior with Justitia enthroned, holding a sword and scales; female allegorical figures looking up to her and two putti holding shields. A formal composition of Justice as sovereign female judge.
citacao: JUSTITIA enthroned, with sword and scales. Século XVII. 1 gravura. British Museum, London. Disponível em: https://www.britishmuseum.org/collection/object/P_1870-0625-185.

titulo: Lady Justice (F.W. Pomeroy, Old Bailey, London)
descricao: The iconic gold statue of Lady Justice atop the Old Bailey, London's Central Criminal Court. Notably unblindfolded — holding sword and scales, with spiked crown. The courthouse brochures explain that 'her maidenly form is supposed to guarantee her impartiality.' A prime example of how the female body itself is argued to embody legal virtue.
citacao: POMEROY, Frederick William. Lady Justice. 1907. 1 escultura em bronze dourado. Central Criminal Court (Old Bailey), London. Disponível em: http://www.speel.me.uk/sculptlondon/oldbaileyjustice.htm.

titulo: Providence and Justice (female personifications in niches)
descricao: Providence and Justice, female personifications, whole-length, in niches, with attributes (ruler and scales, snake entwined around arm). Justice paired with Providence in architectural niches — the virtues as guardians of civic space.
citacao: PROVIDENCE and Justice (personificações femininas em nichos). [s.d.]. 1 gravura. British Museum, London. Disponível em: https://www.britishmuseum.org/collection/object/P_1862-0712-304.

titulo: Woman holding sword and scales of justice (Jurisprudentia)
descricao: Engraving by Étienne Delaune (c. 1560) depicting a female figure holding a sword and scales — the canonical attributes of Justice — identified as Jurisprudentia. Delaune was a French goldsmith and engraver active in mid-16th century France and Germany; this work, held at the Wellcome Collection, exemplifies Renaissance allegorical personification of law and jurisprudence.
citacao: DELAUNE, Étienne. Woman holding sword and scales of justice (Jurisprudentia). c. 1560. 1 gravura. Wellcome Collection, London. Disponível em: https://wellcomecollection.org/works/rkvhanzr.

titulo: Women of Britain Say 'Go!'
descricao: British WWI recruitment poster by E.J. Kealey (1915), published by the Parliamentary Recruiting Committee (Poster No. 75). Shows two women and a boy at an open window watching soldiers march away. The female figures embody a gendered allegory of domestic Britain urging men to enlist — deploying the moral authority of womanhood as surrogate for national female personification. One of the most iconic British WWI images, widely reproduced and analysed in the history of gender and war propaganda.
citacao: KEALEY, Ernest James. Women of Britain say 'Go!'. 1915. 1 litografia colorida (cartaz). Imperial War Museum, London. Disponível em: https://www.iwm.org.uk/collections/item/object/14592.

titulo: Gerechtigkeitsbrunnen mit Plastik der Justitia (Hans Gieng, 1543)
descricao: The Gerechtigkeitsbrunnen (Fountain of Justice) in Bern — the first known blindfolded representation of Lady Justice (1543). Hans Gieng's statue holds sword and scales, blindfolded, standing atop vanquished figures representing worldly power (pope, sultan, emperor, magistrate). A foundational image in the history of legal iconography.
citacao: GIENG, Hans. Gerechtigkeitsbrunnen mit Plastik der Justitia. 1543. 1 escultura, fonte. Bern, Gerechtigkeitsgasse. Bildarchiv Foto Marburg. Disponível em: https://www.bildindex.de/document/que20183316.

titulo: Allegory with Justizia, crowned by a putto
descricao: Allegorical representation of Justizia resting on clouds, being crowned by a putto (cherub), with scales visible. Justice as a celestial female figure receiving divine coronation — the theological dimension of legal allegory.
citacao: WALDMANN, Kaspar. Allegory with Justizia, resting on a cloudbank, crowned by a putto. [séc. XVIII]. 1 desenho. Albertina Museum. Disponível em: https://www.europeana.eu/en/item/15508/30435.

titulo: Justitia (Hans Kels d. J.)
instituicao: Kunsthistorisches Museum Wien (Fine Arts Museum Vienna)
descricao: Golden gilded relief sculpture depicting Justitia. A Renaissance example of the female allegorical figure of justice rendered in precious materials — connecting legal symbolism to material luxury and sovereign authority.
citacao: KELS, Hans d. J. Justitia. [séc. XVI]. 1 relevo dourado. Kunsthistorisches Museum Wien. Disponível em: https://www.europeana.eu/en/item/15502/KK_6068.

titulo: 50 Mark Reichsbanknote — Germania as Young Girl (Weimar 1919)
Notgeld Bielefeld — Jungbrunnen (Fountain of Youth) silk note
Alegoria da República (Carlos Chambelland)
descricao: Allegorical painting commissioned for Brazil's centennial of independence (1922). Female figure in flowing white and green robes, arm raised, accompanied by an angel, with male figures holding manuscripts below — blending republican allegory with national celebration.
citacao: CHAMBELLAND, Carlos. Alegoria da República. 1922. 1 pintura, óleo sobre tela, 202 x 152 cm. Museu Histórico Nacional. Disponível em: https://brasiliana.museus.gov.br/acervos/alegoria-da-republica/.

titulo: As Estátuas Fallando (The Statues Speaking) — Justiça
descricao: Article titled 'As Estátuas Fallando' (The Statues Speaking) featuring a literary dialogue where Justice appears as an animated statue character. 'A Verdade: Que dizes, ó Justiça?' (Truth: What do you say, oh Justice?). Early Brazilian example of Justice personified as a speaking female figure in periodical press.
citacao: AS ESTÁTUAS fallando. Brasil, n. 60, 1827. p. [s.n.]. Disponível em: http://memoria.bn.br/pdf/702390/per702390_1827_00060.pdf.

titulo: Estátua da Justiça (Luiz Rochet) — referência no Almanak Laemmert
descricao: Reference in the Almanak Laemmert (1929 edition) to the statue of Justice by French sculptor Luiz Rochet, inaugurated September 1872. Alongside statues of History, Poetry and Science. Documents the importation of French allegorical sculpture into Brazilian public space.
citacao: ALMANAK Laemmert. Rio de Janeiro, 1929. ed. A00085, p. 542. Disponível em: http://memoria.bn.br/docreader/webindex/WIPagina/313394/101306.

titulo: Allégorie de la Justice (Joseph Stallaert, Brussels City Hall)
instituicao: Musée de la Ville de Bruxelles / Hôtel de Ville
descricao: Allegorical painting of Justice in the Mayor's Cabinet of Brussels City Hall, by Joseph Stallaert. Part of a triptych with Charity and Foresight — three female allegorical virtues required of a mayor. Justice symbolized by scales and sword. A Belgian civic example connecting female virtue to political authority.
citacao: STALLAERT, Joseph. Allégorie de la Justice. 1885–1888. 1 pintura (teto). Hôtel de Ville, Bruxelles. Disponível em: https://collections.heritage.brussels/fr/objects/36822.

titulo: [Alegoría de la República española y la República francesa]
descricao: Allegorical drawing or print depicting the Spanish Republic and the French Republic as paired female figures. Produced in the context of the proclamation of the Second Spanish Republic (April 14, 1931), evoking the fraternal bond between the two republican nations. Part of the BNE's collection of drawings, engravings, and photographs relating to the Second Republic. The Spanish Republic figure wears the Phrygian cap and holds attributes of justice and liberty, mirroring the Marianne iconotype of France.
citacao: ALEGORÍA de la República española y la República francesa. 1931. 1 desenho/gravura. Biblioteca Nacional de España, Madrid. Disponível em: https://bdh.bne.es/bnesearch/detalle/76944.

titulo: Electricity Presenting Light to the World ($5 Silver Certificate)
Illustrations de Calendrier et compost des bergiers (Danse macabre — female Justice figure)
descricao: Illustrations from the 'Calendar and Almanac of Shepherds' featuring the dance of death and allegorical figures including Justice. An early representation of Justice as a female figure within the memento mori tradition — the intersection of death, morality, and law.
citacao: CALENDRIER et compost des bergiers: La grant danse macabre. 1531. Manuscrito ilustrado. Bibliothèque nationale de France. Disponível em: https://gallica.bnf.fr/.

titulo: A Republica precisa de ti: vota em Norton de Matos
autoria: Comissão Distrital do Porto da Candidatura
descricao: Electoral poster from the 1949 Portuguese presidential campaign featuring a female allegorical figure representing the Portuguese Republic. 'The Republic needs you: vote for Norton de Matos.' A classical female figure in robes symbolizes the Republic during a period of authoritarian governance — the allegory deployed as opposition to dictatorship.
citacao: A REPUBLICA precisa de ti: vota em Norton de Matos. 1949. 1 cartaz. Comissão Distrital do Porto. Biblioteca Nacional de Portugal. Disponível em: https://bndigital.bnportugal.gov.pt/.

titulo: El Altar de la Patria
instituicao: Museo Departamental de Bellas Artes Juan Manuel Blanes, Montevidéu
descricao: Pintura alegórica de Juan Manuel Blanes, conhecido como 'El pintor de la Patria', na qual o Uruguai é representado como mulher erguida diante de um altar cívico. Blanes construiu para a jovem República Oriental do Uruguai o repertório visual necessário à autocompreensão nacional. O tema do altar da pátria — mulher diante de um monumento votivo com brasão e símbolos da república — dialoga com a tradição europeia da alegoria feminina nacional e com a iconografia liberal sul-americana do século XIX. A obra integra a coleção permanente do Museu Blanes em Montevidéu.
direitos: Domínio público (Juan Manuel Blanes, 1830–1901; obra de 1896)
citacao: BLANES, Juan Manuel. El Altar de la Patria. 1896. 1 pintura (óleo sobre tela). Montevidéu: Museo Departamental de Bellas Artes Juan Manuel Blanes. Disponível em: https://www.museos.gub.uy/index.php/component/k2/item/1550-juan-manuel-blanes-1830-1901. Acesso em: 28 mar. 2026.
